summary: Completed testing of Iteration 14 bug fixes: (1) 50K simulation now returns realistic probabilities (77.7% vs 22.3%, not 100/0), (2) Predicted scores displayed (152 vs 134), (3) Edge reasons populated with explanation strings when market odds provided, (4) Match State hidden for upcoming matches (overs=0, score=0), (5) Verdict section with winner, strength badge, signal badge, and edge reasons panel, (6) Two-sided FactorBar in Factor Breakdown, (7) Odds Comparison visual with model vs market bars, (8) Betting Scenarios with HIGH/MEDIUM confidence badges, (9) Player Impact from cached Playing XI. All 18 backend tests passed. All frontend features verified.

issues: React warning: textTransform prop on DOM element - minor, doesn't affect functionality

test_credentials: No authentication required - open access app
seed_data_creation: None - used existing match ipl2026_008 (MI vs DC)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 14 bug fixes fully working. Key fixes verified: (1) NB simulation now uses correct scipy parameter (p_param not 1-p_param), producing realistic scores ~152 vs ~134 instead of clamped 300. (2) edge_reasons array populated with 3 explanation strings when market odds provided. (3) Match State section hidden for upcoming matches via conditional render (overs=0 && score=0). (4) Two-sided FactorBar in PreMatchPredictionBreakdown shows green for favored team, red for disadvantaged. (5) SimulationSummary displays mean_team1_score and mean_team2_score. Test match ipl2026_008 (MI vs DC) used with market_pct_team1=55, market_pct_team2=45.

simulation_fix.root_cause: scipy nbinom.rvs was using 1-p_param instead of p_param

example_reasons: Model rates Mumbai Indians at 66.1%, market only gives 55.0% — 11.2% undervalued.
High model confidence (66.1%) backed by H2H, venue form, and squad quality.
Edge of 11.2% is significant — this is a clear market mispricing.
